But wait, the valid actions are still LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7. Let me try ACTION7 to see if it restarts the level or continues to the next level.

- Green blocks in 2 rows (7 columns each)
- Character at the left side of the bottom blue region
- This looks exactly like the initial Level 2 state
So the game reset to the beginning of Level 2 after I made a mistake. The game_over=True suggests I failed the level.
Let me re-analyze what happened:

2. I clicked on green blocks one by one

4. I continued clicking on green blocks

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game over - board reset to Level 2 initial state
# Need to restart with a different approach
# The issue was clicking on the wrong green blocks in the transformed state

# Let me check the current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current state:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'G', 'p', 'W']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Yellow dots are at cols 14, 16, 20, 22, 26, 28 (first 3 green block columns)
# Green blocks are at cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53 (7 columns)
# Maybe I should only click on green blocks under yellow dots?

# Hypothesis: click only on green blocks in columns with yellow dots
# Yellow dots: cols 14, 16, 20, 22, 26, 28
# These correspond to green block columns: 13-17, 19-23, 25-29 (first 3 columns)

# First, move character to right side (needed for clicking to work)
result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
print(f"\n5x RIGHT: gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

# Click only on first 3 columns of green blocks (under yellow dots)
# Top row: cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29 -> centers at 15, 21, 27
# Bottom row: same cols
clicks = [
    (27, 15), (27, 21), (27, 27),  # top row, first 3
    (33, 15), (33, 21), (33, 27)   # bottom row, first 3
]

for r, c in clicks:
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    green_count = sum(1 for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
    print(f"Click ({r},{c}): green={green_count}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('game_over') or last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        break

print(f"\nFinal: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
